styleBrief.text: Hand-drawn graphite and light ink on warm off-white paper, with restrained gray wash and subtle sepia warmth around metal and fire. Serious 1452 siege staging that reads clearly at thumbnail size.

palette.name: Bronze
palette.meaning: Restrained accent on metal and fire.

lineAndFinish.text: Confident dark contour for silhouettes and major props, with lighter exploratory construction marks inside the form. Folds, masonry cracks, and smoke stay in thinner graphite.
lineAndFinish.hatching: Sparse cross-hatch in shadow only; let paper tooth show.

valueAndAccent.text: Five-step value range with high contrast. Simple shadow blocks keep silhouettes readable against stone and smoke.

accents.name: Bronze
accents.meaning: Allowed only on metal and fire.

guardrails.text: Favor serious, heavy, plausible siege imagery; avoid anything that weakens readable staging.
